comments.body: Claiming a narrow docs-routing sync from current main. Scope: add a dedicated central proof-gap index row for the AGL23/BDG24 counting-extraction barrier residual. This will not prove the extraction theorem family.

Docs-routing follow-up pushed to `main` in `6ff921ecf` (`docs: route ABF proximity gaps`).

Added central proof-gap index row: AGL23/BDG24 counting-extraction barrier residual -> #96.

Validation:
- `git diff --check -- docs/kb/audits/ARKLIB-PROOF-GAP-INDEX-2026-06-06.md`
- batch anchor scan for #92/#93/#94/#95/#96/#99/#100/#101/#102/#103/#104/#106 rows

Leaving this open: the extraction theorem family remains unsolved; this only adds central ownership/routing.

Claiming a precise, self-contained arithmetic/combinatorial brick toward `AGL23CountingExtraction`, NOT the full extraction theorem.

**Brick: bounded-fiber restriction counting** (the §3.3 pigeonhole engine, isolated as pure combinatorics). Statement: for a finite codeword set `S : Finset (ι → F)` and a window `I₀ : Finset ι`, if the `I₀`-restriction map has every fiber of size `≤ m` (i.e. at most `m` words of `S` share any given restriction), then the restriction image has card `≥ |S| / m`, equivalently `|S| ≤ m · |image|`. This is the load-bearing step that turns a per-window collision bound (the list-decodability consequence: at most ℓ codewords agree on a near-complementary coordinate set ⟹ bounded fibers) into the large family `Fam` consumed by `alphabet_barrier_reduction`.

Scratch file: `ArkLib/ToMathlib/AGL23CountingProof.lean`. Will land green + axiom-clean, then wire a `Fam`-lower-bound corollary feeding `alphabet_barrier_reduction`. The full counting inequality (`2^{cn} ≤ q^{4ηn}`) needs the unformalized AGL23 Lemma 4.1/4.2 + q-ary Plotkin bound and remains external; I will post an honest status on exactly what this brick covers vs. what remains.

**Brick landed: bounded-fiber restriction counting (AGL23 §3.3 pigeonhole core).**

Pushed to `main` in `a3d2263df`. New file `ArkLib/ToMathlib/AGL23CountingProof.lean`, five lemmas, all sorry-free and axiom-clean.

**What (all in `namespace AGL23`):**
- `card_le_mul_image_card_of_fiber_card_le` — if every `I₀`-restriction fiber of a finite codeword set `S` has card `≤ m`, then `|S| ≤ m · |image|`. (fiber-partition + `Finset.card_eq_sum_card_fiberwise`.)
- `image_card_ge_of_fiber_card_le` — `ℕ` form `|S| / m ≤ |image|`.
- `image_card_ge_real_of_fiber_card_le` — `ℝ`-cast form `(|S| : ℝ)/m ≤ |image|` (no floor; chains into `2^(c·n)`).
- `exists_fam_of_fiber_bound` — produces the `(Fam, hFam_lower : 2^(c·n) ≤ |Fam|, hrealize : Fam ⊆ S.image (·|I₀))` triple in the EXACT shape `alphabet_barrier_reduction` consumes, from `2^(c·n) ≤ |S|/m`.
- `alphabet_barrier_of_fiber_bound` — end-to-end bridge: window `|I₀| ≤ 4εn` + fiber bound `m` + `2^(c·n) ≤ |S|/m` ⟹ `q ≥ 2^((c/4)/ε)`, by composing with `alphabet_barrier_reduction`.

**Evidence:** `lake env lean ArkLib/ToMathlib/AGL23CountingProof.lean` exit 0, zero warnings. In-file `#print axioms` for all five = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` (no `sorryAx`).

**What this covers:** the pure-combinatorial pigeonhole engine that converts a per-window collision bound into the large family `Fam`. It discharges the `(S, I₀, Fam)`-construction interface of the existing reduction, *conditional on* a per-fiber collision bound `m` and a source-set size lower bound.

**What remains external (genuinely unformalized AGL23 §4):**
1. **Lemma 4.1** (subcode-with-distance): from rank ≥ ρn + list-decodability at `ℓ/(ℓ+1)(1-ρ-η)`, extract the source set `S` and the per-fiber collision bound `m ≤ ℓ` (this is where the list-decodability hypothesis is actually consumed — it bounds how many codewords share a near-complementary restriction).
2. **Lemma 4.2** (large set family / window selection): manufacture the window `I₀` with `|I₀| ≤ 4ηn` and certify `2^(c·n) ≤ |S|/m` with an explicit `c > 0`.
3. The `q`-ary **Plotkin** average-distance upper bound that Lemma 4.1 rests on — absent from mathlib (see disposition note in `external-listdecoding-bounds.md`).

So `AGL23CountingExtraction ℓ ρ` and hence `large_alphabet_barrier_bdg24_agl23` remain external; this brick formalizes and verifies the §3.3 pigeonhole interface they plug into, and is now reusable in-tree. Leaving #96 open.
Claiming this issue and working on it.
